2606.31966.title: MECoBench: A Systematic Study of Multimodal Agent Collaboration in Embodied Environments
2606.31966.paper_summary: Recent multimodal large language models (MLLMs) have strong potential as embodied agents, but their ability to collaborate in visually grounded environments remains underexplored. To address this gap, we introduce MECoBench, a multimodal embodied cooperation benchmark with an evaluation platform spanning diverse real-world tasks, two cooperation structures, and three collaboration modes. Through extensive experiments across various MLLMs, we summarize three key findings: (i) Collaboration generally improves embodied task completion, but its benefits depend on balancing collaborative gains against coordination complexity. (ii) Communication is essential to collaboration gains, while the best collaboration mode depends on team size and model capability. (iii) Moreover, collaboration improves robustness under noisy priors and exploration conditions. Generally, MECoBench provides a systematic testbed for understanding the mechanisms and limits of multimodal embodied collaboration. Code and dataset are available at https://github.com/q-i-n-g/MECoBench.
2606.31966.paper_summary_zh: 最近的多模態大型語言模型（MLLMs）作為具身代理具有強大的潛力，但它們在視覺基礎環境中的協作能力仍然未被充分探索。為了解決這一空白，我們介紹了MECoBench，一個多模態具身合作基準，擁有一個涵蓋多樣化現實世界任務的評估平台、兩種合作結構和三種合作模式。通過對各種MLLMs進行廣泛實驗，我們總結了三個關鍵發現：（i）合作通常能改善具身任務的完成，但其好處取決於平衡合作收益與協調複雜性。（ii）溝通對於合作收益至關重要，而最佳的合作模式則取決於團隊規模和模型能力。（iii）此外，合作在噪聲先驗和探索條件下提高了魯棒性。總體而言，MECoBench提供了一個系統的測試平台，以理解多模態具身合作的機制和限制。代碼和數據集可在 https://github.com/q-i-n-g/MECoBench 獲得。
2606.31966.author: Qingyun Liu et.al.
2606.31966.authors: Qingyun Liu, Jiwen Zhang, Jingyi Hu, Siyuan Wang, Zhongyu Wei
